title: Zephyranthes pumila
taxonomy: Zephyranthes pumila is classified as a species within the Amaryllidaceae family. This family includes over 80 genera and 1,600 species of flowering plants. Zephyranthes pumila itself belongs to the genus Zephyranthes, which consists of approximately 80 species of flowering plants. The genus Zephyranthes is further divided into the subgenus Zephyranthes, and it's a type of flowering plant commonly known as fairy lily or zephyr lily. The exact classification within the Amaryllidaceae family may be subject to revision based on ongoing research.
uses: Zephyranthes pumila has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. Its roots and bulbs are rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In some cultures, Zephyranthes pumila is also used to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Zephyranthes pumila.

preparations: Zephyranthes pumila, also known as the Dwarf Zephyr Lily, has been used in herbal preparations due to its potential medicinal properties. intro: Zephyranthes pumila, commonly known as the dwarf zephyr lily or miniature rain lily, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Amaryllidaceae. The health benefits of Zephyranthes pumila include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments. Bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids such as galanthamine and lycorine, as well as other compounds like 6-hydroxydihydrosanguinarine and 1,4-dihydropyridine alkaloids. In traditional medicine, Zephyranthes pumila is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as respiratory infections and skin disorders.
